text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each merge triggers an automated build, meaning the system compiles the new code instantly. Immediately after, the CI pipeline runs a series of tests to check for errors or bugs. The main goal is catching issues early, before they become bigger problems. If a build fails or a test does not pass, developers get immediate feedback and can fix the problem right away. This process helps teams maintain higher code quality and work together more smoothly, saving time and effort throughout the project.
